channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: 'Dude, good luck': the Olympic wit and wisdom of a pioneering African skeleton racer
item.description: Who says serious athletes are always serious? Akwasi Frimpong, who's competed for Ghana, is a world-class wisecracker as he reflects on being a Black ...

item.title: That ain't perfume! Ancient bottle contained feces, likely used for medicine
item.description: Researchers found a tiny bottle from ancient Rome that contained fecal residue and traces of aromatics, offering evidence that poop was used medicinal...

item.title: Brain organoids can be trained to solve a goal-directed task
item.description: Imagine balancing a ruler vertically in the palm of your hand: you have to constantly pay attention to the angle of the ruler and make many small adju...

item.title: Targeted radiation therapy improves quality of life outcomes for patients with multiple brain metastases, finds study
item.description: In a Phase III randomized trial, Mass General Brigham researchers found that stereotactic radiation targeting individual tumors led to lower symptom b...

item.title: What Nutrients Are GLP-1 Users Lacking?
item.description: In adults with obesity and/or type 2 diabetes, GLP-1 therapy may lead to nutritional imbalances, with growing evidence indicating depletion of vitamin...

item.title: Cognitive Training May Lower Dementia Risk 20 Years Later
item.description: In a long-running RCT, older adults who completed adaptive speed-of-processing training with boosters were less likely to develop dementia — a benefit...

item.title: Fueling for gold: How nutrition impacts strength, speed, and recovery in Olympic athletes
item.description: If our bodies are machines and food is fuel, it is safe to say that elite performance requires high-octane nutrition. As the Winter Olympics continue ...

item.title: Digital Coaching Can Help Control Blood Glucose in T2D
item.description: A diabetes management system with personalized remote coaching can improve glycemic control to a greater extent than usual care in patients with T2D. ...

item.title: Four States and New York City Join WHO Outbreak Network
item.description: After United States’ withdrawal from the WHO, four states and New York City have partnered with the WHO to maintain access to international outbreak i...

item.title: High Fracture Risk Persists in Kidney Transplant Recipients
item.description: A notable proportion of kidney transplant recipients experience fractures, with rates being unchanged over 20 years and a high imminent risk for subse...

item.title: Red blood cells soak up sugar at high altitude, protecting against diabetes
item.description: Scientists have long known that people living at high altitudes, where oxygen levels are low, have lower rates of diabetes than people living closer t...

item.title: Same-day hospital discharge found to be safe for select patients after transcatheter aortic valve implantation
item.description: With careful selection, same-day hospital discharge was found to be feasible and safe in around one-fifth of patients undergoing transcatheter aortic ...

item.title: Early screening saves limbs from silent vascular disease [PODCAST]
item.description: Subscribe to The Podcast by KevinMD. Watch on YouTube. Catch up on old episodes! Postdoctoral researcher Wei Zhang discusses the article “Peripheral a...

item.title: Learning from patients: How a physician gained strength and resilience
item.description: I remember. It is a gorgeous summer day as the sunlight streaks through the redwood canopy. My grip clenches the dangling rope. My stance wobbles on t...

item.title: Zuckerberg grilled about Meta's strategy to target 'teens' and 'tweens'
item.description: The billionaire tech mogul's testimony was part of a landmark social media addiction trial in Los Angeles. The jury's verdict in the case could shape ...

item.title: FDA reverses course on Moderna flu shot
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration's about-face comes a little more than a week after the agency refused to consider the company's application to market...

item.title: Sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 inhibitors may improve left ventricular diastolic function in hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
item.description: 1. Sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 inhibitors (SGLT-2i) were associated with improved left ventricular diastolic function and New York Heart Associatio...

item.title: This form of mental exercise may cut dementia risk for decades
item.description: A study finds that people who did one specific form of brain training in the 1990s were less likely to be diagnosed with dementia over the next 20 yea...

item.title: FDA clears Medtronic stealth AXiS AI-Robotic spine system
item.description: 1. The Stealth AXiS™ system has received regulatory clearance as the first platform to natively unify AI-driven planning and robotic execution for spi...

item.title: The “ethical canary”: How moral injury signals systemic failure
item.description: For the past month and maybe longer, I have been waking up with pressure in my chest. It worsened in December and continued to worsen in the new year....

item.title: Rural emergency medicine in New Mexico: a physician’s firsthand account
item.description: Recently, I was asked to write about my experience as a rural emergency physician in New Mexico. The timing was uncanny: I am in the final shifts of m...

item.title: Beyond Flexner: Why we must rethink medical training reform
item.description: Our current scheme and its overbearing credentialism is out of date and cannot keep up with a rapidly changing health care environment. Greater flexib...

item.title: Exposure to air pollution is associated with increased risk of developing limitations in physical function
item.description: 1. Greater long-term exposure to particulate matter (PM) and nitrogen dioxide (NO2) was associated with increased risk of progressing from no disabili...

item.title: One in Six Medicare Beneficiaries Use Telehealth for Essential Care
item.description: 1. In this cross-sectional study, 16.4% of Medicare beneficiaries had at least one telehealth visit between 2021 and 2023, with users more likely to h...

item.title: 2MM: AI Roundup- Medtronic’s AI-Robotic Spine Platform, 90% Phase I Success for AI-Designed Drugs, and UCSD’s AI Cancer Risk Predictor [Feb 17th, 2026]
